name: Vladimir Putin
said: Russia does not attack civilian infrastructure.
did: Full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Feb 2022 — no UN authorization.
Hundreds of thousands of casualties (military + civilian) documented by UN OHCHR.
Systematic targeting of civilian infrastructure — hospitals, schools, power grid.
Forced deportation of Ukrainian children — ICC arrest warrant issued March 2023.
Blocking humanitarian access to besieged cities (Mariupol).
Assassination and poisoning of political opponents (Navalny, Litvinenko, Skripal).
Personal wealth estimated $70-200B despite official salary of ~$140K/year.
facts: ICC arrest warrant for war crimes — deportation of children (March 2023).
UN General Assembly resolution condemning invasion (141 countries in favor).
OHCHR documented 10,000+ civilian casualties in first year alone.
Bucha massacre documented by satellite imagery and forensic evidence.
Navalny died in prison February 2024 after documenting Putin's $1.35B palace.
Novichok nerve agent used on Skripal family in UK — OPCW confirmed.
Constitutional changes 2020 to allow rule until 2036.
Jailed journalists — Russia ranked 164/180 press freedom (RSF).
Wagner Group mercenaries deployed in Syria, Africa — documented war crimes.
Election manipulation documented by independent monitors (OSCE).
sources: International Criminal Court — arrest warrant ICC-01/22
UN OHCHR civilian casualty reports
OPCW Technical Assessment — Skripal poisoning
Navalny Anti-Corruption Foundation — palace investigation
OSCE election monitoring reports
Reporters Without Borders press freedom index
Satellite imagery (Maxar Technologies) — Bucha
UN General Assembly Resolution ES-11/1

name: Recep Tayyip Erdogan
said: Turkey is a democracy that respects the rule of law.
did: Jailed more journalists than any other country (CPJ, multiple years running).
Post-2016 coup purge: 150,000+ people detained, fired, or arrested.
Constitutional referendum 2017 — concentrated power in presidency.
Currency crisis: Turkish lira lost 80%+ value 2018-2023 under his economic policies.
Family members in key positions — son-in-law was Finance Minister.
Seized independent media outlets — now state-controlled or allied-owned.
facts: Turkey holds world record for jailed journalists (CPJ annual reports 2016-2022).
European Court of Human Rights — hundreds of rulings against Turkey for rights violations.
Corruption probe 2013 — wiretaps showed ministers discussing bribes; Erdogan shut down investigation, reassigned prosecutors.
Nepotism: son-in-law Berat Albayrak served as Finance Minister 2018-2020.
Post-coup purge: 160,000+ passports cancelled, 50,000+ arrested (Amnesty International).
Press freedom: Turkey ranked 165/180 (RSF 2023).
Erdogan's declared wealth increased significantly during time in office.
Military operations in Syria — civilian casualties documented by UN.
sources: Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) annual prison census
Amnesty International Turkey reports
European Court of Human Rights case database
Reporters Without Borders press freedom index
Turkish government financial disclosures
UN OHCHR reports on northeast Syria operations
OECD anti-corruption reviews
2013 corruption probe wiretap transcripts (leaked, widely reported)

name: Donald Trump
said: I will fight for the working people.
I have never met that woman.
did: 34 felony convictions — falsifying business records (NY, May 2024).
Found liable for sexual abuse and defamation — $83.3M judgment (E. Jean Carroll case).
Tax cuts primarily benefiting top 1% — Congressional Budget Office analysis.
Four criminal indictments across multiple jurisdictions.
January 6th Capitol breach after rally speech — impeached for incitement.
Documented association with Jeffrey Epstein — photos, flight records, public statements.
Family members in White House positions — Jared Kushner, Ivanka Trump (nepotism).
facts: 34 felony convictions — People of New York v. Trump (2024).
Civil fraud judgment: $454M for inflating asset values (NY AG case).
E. Jean Carroll: found liable for sexual abuse by federal jury.
Two impeachments by House of Representatives (2019, 2021).
January 6 Committee: referred for criminal prosecution on 4 charges.
Documented 30,000+ false or misleading claims during presidency (Washington Post tracker).
Trump Foundation shut down by NY AG for 'shocking pattern of illegality.'
Classified documents found at Mar-a-Lago — federal indictment.
Kushner received $2B from Saudi sovereign wealth fund after leaving White House.
On Epstein: 'I've known Jeff for fifteen years. Terrific guy.' (2002, NY Magazine).
sources: NY County Supreme Court — People v. Trump (34 felony convictions)
US District Court SDNY — Carroll v. Trump (sexual abuse, defamation)
NY Supreme Court — AG v. Trump Organization ($454M civil fraud)
US House January 6th Committee Final Report

name: Benjamin Netanyahu
said: We act in accordance with international law.
We take every precaution to avoid civilian casualties.
did: ICC arrest warrant issued November 2024 for war crimes and crimes against humanity.
Gaza operation 2023-2024: 30,000+ civilian casualties documented by UN.
Blocking humanitarian aid — UN reported famine conditions.
Ongoing corruption trial: bribery, fraud, and breach of trust (Case 4000, Case 1000, Case 2000).
Judicial overhaul attempt to weaken courts while under indictment.
facts: ICC arrest warrant — war crimes, crimes against humanity (Nov 2024).
UN OCHA: over 35,000 Palestinians killed including 13,000+ children by mid-2024.
ICJ ruled plausible risk of genocide — ordered provisional measures (Jan 2024).
Corruption trial ongoing since 2020: bribery (Case 4000), fraud (Case 1000, 2000).
UN: 70%+ of housing in Gaza destroyed or damaged.
Amnesty International: documented use of white phosphorus in populated areas.
Multiple UN votes condemning settlement expansion — violations of international law.
Attempted judicial overhaul while under criminal indictment.
sources: International Criminal Court — arrest warrant (Nov 2024)
International Court of Justice — provisional measures order (Jan 2024)

name: Mohammed bin Salman (MBS)
role: Crown Prince / De facto ruler of Saudi Arabia
said: Saudi Arabia is modernizing and reforming.
I had nothing to do with the Khashoggi killing.
did: Jamal Khashoggi murdered and dismembered in Saudi consulate, Istanbul, Oct 2018.
UN investigation concluded 'credible evidence' MBS was responsible.
US intelligence assessment: MBS approved Khashoggi operation (ODNI report released 2021).
Mass execution of 81 people in single day (March 2022) — largest in Saudi history.
Imprisoned women's rights activists who campaigned for right to drive.
Yemen war: coalition airstrikes killed thousands of civilians.
facts: UN Special Rapporteur Callamard: 'credible evidence warranting investigation of MBS' for Khashoggi murder.
US ODNI declassified report: 'MBS approved an operation to capture or kill Khashoggi.'
Saudi agents who killed Khashoggi included MBS personal security team.
Women's rights activist Loujain al-Hathloul imprisoned 2018-2021 for campaigning.
Yemen coalition airstrikes: 19,000+ killed including 4,000+ children (UNICEF/UN data through 2022).
Ritz-Carlton 'anti-corruption' purge 2017: detained 200+ businesspeople, extracted ~$100B (Wall Street Journal).
Neom megaproject: forced displacement of Huwaitat tribe, at least 1 killed resisting (Amnesty).
Saudi Arabia: 196 executions in 2022, 172 in 2023 (Amnesty International).
sources: UN Special Rapporteur report on Khashoggi killing (A/HRC/41/CRP.1)
US ODNI declassified assessment (released Feb 2021)
Amnesty International annual reports — Saudi Arabia
UNICEF Yemen situation reports
Wall Street Journal — Ritz-Carlton detentions reporting
Human Rights Watch Saudi Arabia reports
Turkish prosecutor investigation files (leaked portions)

name: Xi Jinping
said: China respects human rights with Chinese characteristics.
We will peacefully reunify with Taiwan.
did: Mass detention of Uyghur Muslims — UN estimated 1 million+ in camps.
Forced sterilization, forced labor, cultural erasure documented by multiple UN bodies.
Crushing of Hong Kong democracy movement — National Security Law 2020.
Zero independent media — China ranked 179/180 press freedom (RSF).
Eliminated presidential term limits 2018 — indefinite rule.
Disappeared critics: tennis star Peng Shuai, business leaders, lawyers.
facts: UN OHCHR report (Aug 2022): 'serious human rights violations' against Uyghurs, possible 'crimes against humanity.'
Xinjiang Police Files (2022): leaked internal documents showing mass detention system.
Uyghur Tribunal (independent, UK-based): concluded genocide, Dec 2021.
Hong Kong: 260+ arrested under National Security Law, pro-democracy media shut down.
Forced labor in Xinjiang: cotton, solar panels — documented by ASPI, Sheffield Hallam U.
Disappearances: Peng Shuai (tennis star, 2021), Jack Ma (weeks, 2020), human rights lawyers (ongoing).
Press freedom: 127 journalists jailed (CPJ 2022) — world's worst jailer.
Constitutional amendment removed term limits (2018) — president for life.
sources: UN OHCHR Assessment — Xinjiang (Aug 31, 2022)
Xinjiang Police Files — leaked documents (2022)
Uyghur Tribunal judgment (Dec 2021)
Australian Strategic Policy Institute (ASPI) Xinjiang research
Committee to Protect Journalists — China prison census
Reporters Without Borders press freedom index
Hong Kong police arrest records under NSL
Sheffield Hallam University — forced labor in solar supply chain

name: Bashar al-Assad
said: There are no chemical weapons attacks.
did: Chemical weapons attacks on own civilians — confirmed by OPCW and UN.
Ghouta sarin attack 2013: 1,400+ killed including hundreds of children.
Barrel bombs dropped on hospitals, schools, markets — systematic targeting of civilians.
Caesar photos: 11,000+ images of systematic torture deaths in government prisons.
500,000+ killed in civil war. 13 million displaced (half the population).
facts: OPCW Investigation and Identification Team: confirmed Syrian government responsibility for chemical attacks.
Caesar photos: 55,000 images smuggled out by military defector, verified by FBI.
UN Commission of Inquiry: documented crimes against humanity, war crimes.
Siege warfare: starvation of civilian populations in Madaya, Eastern Ghouta, Yarmouk.
Enforced disappearances: 100,000+ detainees (Syrian Network for Human Rights).
Destruction of hospitals: 601 attacks on medical facilities documented (Physicians for Human Rights).
After fall 2024: mass graves discovered, torture facilities documented.
sources: OPCW Investigation and Identification Team reports
UN Commission of Inquiry on Syria — multiple reports
Caesar photos — FBI verification, Holocaust Memorial Museum analysis
Physicians for Human Rights — attacks on medical facilities database
Syrian Network for Human Rights documentation
UN OCHA displacement and casualty figures

name: Kim Jong-un
said: The people are prosperous under our system.
did: Political prison camps holding 80,000-120,000 people — documented by satellite and defectors.
Public executions including by anti-aircraft guns.
Ordered assassination of half-brother Kim Jong-nam with VX nerve agent (2017).
Three generations of punishment — families imprisoned for one member's perceived disloyalty.
Famine and malnutrition while spending billions on nuclear weapons and luxury goods.
facts: UN Commission of Inquiry (2014): crimes against humanity 'without parallel in the contemporary world.'
Political prison camps: Camp 14, 15, 16, 18, 25 documented by satellite imagery and defector testimony.
Kim Jong-nam assassination: VX nerve agent, Kuala Lumpur airport, Feb 2017 — Malaysian court case.
Estimated 60% of population food insecure (UN World Food Programme).
Nuclear weapons program: 6 nuclear tests, dozens of missile tests violating UN resolutions.
Zero press freedom — ranked 180/180, dead last (RSF).
Estimated $2-4B in luxury goods imports while population starves (UN Panel of Experts).
Three generations of collective punishment documented by UN COI.
sources: UN Commission of Inquiry report (2014) — A/HRC/25/CRP.1
UN Panel of Experts — DPRK sanctions monitoring
HRNK (Committee for Human Rights in North Korea) — satellite analysis
Malaysian court records — Kim Jong-nam assassination
Reporters Without Borders — press freedom index
Defector testimonies compiled by UN COI
World Food Programme — DPRK food security assessments

name: Alexander Lukashenko
did: 2020 election: widely documented fraud — EU, US, and most international observers rejected results.
Mass arrests: 35,000+ detained during 2020 protests (Viasna Human Rights Centre).
Systematic torture of detainees — documented by UN, Amnesty, HRW.
Forced Ryanair flight to land to arrest dissident journalist Roman Protasevich (2021).
Shut down all independent media, imprisoned journalists.
Enabled Russian invasion of Ukraine from Belarusian territory.
facts: OSCE: 2020 election 'not free and fair' — EU imposed sanctions.
Viasna Human Rights Centre: 35,000+ detained, 1,500+ cases of torture documented.
Nobel laureate Svetlana Alexievich forced to flee.
Forced landing of Ryanair FR4978 — ICAO investigation confirmed state-directed.
All independent media shut down by 2022 — Tut.by, Belsat, Nasha Niva raided/closed.
UN Human Rights Council: documented 'widespread and systematic' torture.
In power since 1994 — 'Europe's last dictator' per international consensus.
sources: OSCE/ODIHR election observation reports
Viasna Human Rights Centre documentation
UN Human Rights Council resolution and reports on Belarus
ICAO investigation — Ryanair FR4978 incident

EU Council sanctions decisions

name: Sackler Family / Purdue Pharma
said: OxyContin is not addictive when used as directed.
We are part of the solution to the opioid crisis.
did: Aggressively marketed OxyContin knowing it was highly addictive.
Internal documents show they knew about addiction risk and pushed sales harder.
500,000+ Americans dead from opioid overdoses (CDC, 1999-2020).
Purdue pleaded guilty to federal criminal charges — TWICE (2007 and 2020).
Sackler family extracted $10.7 billion from Purdue while company fueled crisis.
Moved billions offshore to shield personal wealth from lawsuits.
facts: Purdue Pharma guilty plea 2007: 'misbranding' OxyContin, $600M fine.
Purdue Pharma guilty plea 2020: conspiracy to defraud the US, $8.3B settlement.
DOJ: Purdue 'knowingly and intentionally conspired and agreed to aid and abet' illegal distribution.
Sackler family transferred $10.7B out of Purdue over a decade (court findings in bankruptcy).
CDC: 500,000+ opioid overdose deaths 1999-2020 in US alone.
McKinsey consulting advice to Purdue: 'turbocharge' OxyContin sales (leaked documents).
Internal Purdue memo (1996): 'We do not want to niche OxyContin just for cancer pain.'
Multiple state AG lawsuits — settled for combined $6B+ (Sackler personal contribution).
sources: US DOJ — Purdue Pharma guilty pleas (2007, 2020)
Purdue Pharma bankruptcy court filings — asset transfer findings
CDC WONDER database — opioid overdose mortality data
State AG complaint filings (Massachusetts, New York, etc.)
Internal Purdue documents released in litigation
McKinsey settlement documents
Congressional investigation records (House Oversight Committee)
